steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects Salim's stated intention (demonstrate revision's value, encourage audiences) to her specific action (being transparent with annotations). The transition must logically connect a purpose to the action that carries it out.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Thus,’: "Thus" signals that what follows is a result or logical consequence of what came before. Salim's transparency with annotations is a direct result of her intention to demonstrate revision's value. This connection is correct. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Nevertheless,’: "Nevertheless" signals contrast or concession—it implies she acted despite something standing in the way. Nothing in the passage establishes any obstacle. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Instead,’: "Instead" implies a replacement or reversal—she did X in place of Y. The passage describes no abandoned plan. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Later,’: "Later" marks a time gap between the intention and the action. The passage presents them as directly connected, not separated in time. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
